qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: PF4J ZIP plugin extraction permits Zip Slip arbitrary file write
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: It identifies the same vulnerable code and guard as the advisory: `org.pf4j.util.Unzip.extract()` accepts attacker-controlled zip entry names and validates them with a raw canonical-path string prefix check instead of normalized path-component validation, allowing escaped sibling writes with matching prefixes.

selected_finding.title: PF4J ZIP plugin extraction permits Zip Slip arbitrary file write

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The accepted PF4J finding matches the advisory root cause in `Unzip.extract()`, and the copied `.judge` PoC demonstrated the advisory impact by writing attacker-controlled content to `/tmp/pf4j-zipslip/plugins/victim_escape/payload.txt` outside the intended extraction directory `/tmp/pf4j-zipslip/plugins/victim`.
evidence: The advisory JSON describes a path traversal or Zip Slip in `extract()` of `Unzip.java` caused by improper handling of zip entry names and missing normalization/validation.

The finding report at `/Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-DryRun/advisory_b8163f6b/pf4j_zip_slip_arbitrary_file_write/vulnerability_report.json` points to the same sink and explains that `Unzip.extract()` uses `file.getCanonicalPath().startsWith(destination.getCanonicalPath())` before writing the entry.

The upstream fix in commit `20c2f80089d1ea779e22c2de5f109a0bce4e1b14` replaces the string-prefix guard with normalized `Path.startsWith()` validation and adds sibling-prefix bypass tests, which aligns with the finding's described root cause.

The copied PoC execution log at `/Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-DryRun/advisory_b8163f6b/.judge/poc_runs/pf4j_zip_slip_arbitrary_file_write/run.log` shows `malicious entry: ../victim_escape/payload.txt`, `escaped path: /tmp/pf4j-zipslip/plugins/victim_escape/payload.txt`, `outside destination: true`, and `verification passed`.

The copied PoC adaptation note at `/Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-DryRun/advisory_b8163f6b/.judge/poc_runs/pf4j_zip_slip_arbitrary_file_write/adaptation_notes.txt` records the `.judge`-local change used to avoid the bundle's incidental `mkdirs(..)` failure while exercising the same advisory-fixed sibling-prefix bug.

poc_executions.observed_effect: The copied PoC built and ran the Java harness, and the log shows a crafted `../victim_escape/payload.txt` entry produced `/tmp/pf4j-zipslip/plugins/victim_escape/payload.txt` outside `/tmp/pf4j-zipslip/plugins/victim` with the proof string `EVOHUNT_ZIP_SLIP_PROOF`.
